data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats SC only when the critic operates as a question-adaptive threshold discriminator (rejecting only high-confidence-wrong candidates), not as a fixed global classifier; the decisive regime variable is critic threshold-calibration slope, not marginal precision.", "mechanism": "A fixed-precision critic is a single-threshold binary classifier: it cannot distinguish 'this candidate is wrong and I am sure' from 'this candidate is wrong and I am guessing.' A confidence-adaptive critic applies a per-candidate threshold, rejecting only when P(wrong | critic_features) > tau. This preserves correct majorities (false flags only on low-confidence judgments, which are kept) while removing wrong candidates the critic is sure about. The mechanism is selection-on-confidence: the critic's posterior over 'wrong' must be bimodal (confident-wrong vs uncertain-right), and the threshold tau must sit in the trough. Under this model, harness beats SC when the confident-wrong mass exceeds the uncertain-wrong mass that SC would tolerate, AND the false-flag rate at tau is below the majority buffer. The v4-pro hard-set null (precision 0.125, recall 1.0) is the failure case: the critic operated at a threshold so low it flagged everything, collapsing to no useful filtering.", "scope": {"covers": ["critics with access to per-candidate confidence or calibrated posterior", "k >= 5", "base error e in (1/(k+1), 1/2)", "bimodal critic-error distributions with a separable confident-wrong mode", "threshold-tunable critic architectures"], "excludes": ["fixed-threshold critics with no confidence output", "critic precision/recall reported as single global numbers without per-question calibration", "unanimous SC pools", "base error e > 1/2"], "forbidden": [{"case": "Experiment with confidence-adaptive critic at k=7, e in [0.10, 0.20] shows harness < SC when critic threshold tau is set to reject all candidates with P(wrong) > 0.05", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment shows harness > SC at k=5, e in [0.04, 0.08] using a fixed-threshold critic with no confidence output and global precision < 0.60", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment shows confidence-adaptive harness gain is not statistically separable from fixed-threshold harness gain when critic posterior is unimodal (no confident-wrong mode)", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "Negates 30e483eb partially by going beyond 'critic confidence is question-adaptive' to specify the causal mechanism: bimodal error posterior + threshold in the trough. The decisive experiment manipulates critic threshold tau across a grid, predicting a U-shaped harness-vs-SC curve: too-low tau (flag everything, precision collapses) and too-high tau (flag nothing, recall collapses) both yield net-zero or negative, while the optimal tau in the posterior trough yields strict gain. Budget: ~240k tokens for sweep."}
